commonMedications.name: Semaglutide/GLP-1 (Ozempic)
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS Medical Card covers for diabetes. DPS scheme caps at €80/month for all drugs.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine/Synthroid (Thyroid)
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS Medical Card: free. DPS scheme: counts toward €80/mo cap.

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (Blood Pressure)
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS/DPS covered; very low cost generic.

commonMedications.name: Metformin (Diabetes)
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS Medical Card: free. Long-term illness scheme covers diabetes.

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. Boots, LloydsPharmacy, and independent pharmacies throughout Waterford city and surrounding towns. Late-night pharmacy available. Pharmacies can provide flu vaccinations and minor ailment consultations.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Prescriptions from GP required. Dispensed at any pharmacy. DPS (Drugs Payment Scheme) caps household drug costs at €80/month. Medical card holders (income-tested, automatic for over-70s earning under €1,050/week) get free prescriptions with €1.50 per item charge. Long Term Illness (LTI) scheme covers specific chronic conditions free.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Private health insurance (VHI, Laya, Irish Life Health) does not typically cover outpatient prescriptions. DPS is the primary cost-capping mechanism. Medical card or GP visit card available based on income/age.

annualExamCost.notes: Eye exams €40-60 at opticians (Specsavers, Vision Express). Some private health plans cover annual eye test. Medical card holders get free eye exams through HSE.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Some private health plans (VHI, Laya) include optical benefit — typically €50-75 toward glasses every 2 years. PRSI Treatment Benefit Scheme provides free eye exam and €42 toward glasses for those with sufficient PRSI contributions.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 2-4 weeks for routine optician appointments in Waterford. Ophthalmologist referral through HSE can take 6-18 months. Private ophthalmologists seen within 2-4 weeks.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Over-70s with medical card get free eye exams. Cataract surgery covered by HSE but long public waiting lists (12-24 months). Private cataract surgery €2,500-3,500 per eye, covered by most private health insurance plans.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Dental cleaning €65-95 in Waterford. Some private plans cover one annual cleaning. PRSI Treatment Benefit covers one free annual exam (not cleaning).

commonProcedures.coverageNote: Not covered by most private plans. PRSI covers basic fillings for eligible contributors.
Some private plans cover 50%. Long wait if through HSE dental service.
Partially covered by some private plans.
PRSI Treatment Benefit covers extractions for eligible contributors.
Not covered by HSE. Some premium private plans partially cover.
Medical card holders eligible for HSE denture service. Long waiting lists.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Irish dental insurance is limited. VHI DeCare dental plans offer annual cleanings and basic coverage for €20-40/month. PRSI Treatment Benefit provides one free annual exam and some treatments for workers with sufficient contributions. Medical card holders get basic dental through HSE.
dental.dentistAvailability: Good availability in Waterford city. Several private practices and dental clinics. HSE dental service has long waiting lists. Smiles Dental and independent practices accept most plans.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Early bird menus common (€20-28 for 2-3 courses). No tipping expected (10% appreciated for excellent service).
categories.notes: Lunch/dinner for 2 at moderate restaurant ~€50-60 (VAT 13.5% included, no tip obligation), 2x/week = ~€280/mo

categories.name: Broadband (SIRO 1Gig)
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior discounts. SIRO fiber available in Waterford.
categories.notes: Vodafone Gigabit Broadband €50-60/mo via SIRO fiber network. Eir Fibre also available. No data caps.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Waterford City Council leisure centres offer senior rates. Kingfisher Club, WIT Arena.

categories.name: Pubs/Culture
categories.seniorDiscounts: OAP (Old Age Pensioner) discounts at some venues and events.
categories.notes: Traditional Irish pub culture. Live music sessions (trad). Theatre Royal Waterford, Waterford Museum of Treasures.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Waterford City Library free membership. Borrowing free.
categories.notes: Public library system excellent. Book clubs, digital lending (BorrowBox).

monthlyPass.notes: Free Travel Pass (FTP) available for all Irish residents aged 66+ — covers Bus Éireann, Irish Rail, Dublin Bus, Luas nationwide. Under-66 Bus Éireann monthly pass ~€80.

publicTransit.coverage: Bus Éireann serves Waterford city and regional routes. Limited frequency outside peak hours. Irish Rail connects Waterford to Dublin Heuston (2hr 10min). No local rail/tram.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Free Travel Pass at age 66 covers all public transport nationwide. One of the best senior transit benefits in Europe.

rideShare.availability: FreeNow (mytaxi) available in Waterford. Uber not widely operational in Ireland outside Dublin. Local taxi services reliable.
rideShare.notes: Taxi regulated by NTA. Fixed airport transfer rates available.

registration.notes: Vehicle Registration Tax (VRT) applies to imported vehicles — significant cost (up to 37% of value). Motor tax based on CO2 emissions, €120-2,350/year depending on vehicle.

insurance.notes: Irish car insurance historically expensive. Named driver experience from abroad may not transfer. Shop around — quotes vary wildly. Liberty Insurance, AXA, Aviva.

fuel.notes: Petrol ~€1.65-1.80/liter, diesel ~€1.55-1.70/liter. Carbon tax increasing annually. Typical senior drives 10,000-15,000 km/year.

nct.notes: National Car Test (NCT) required for roadworthiness. Test centres in Waterford.

parking.notes: Parking affordable compared to Dublin. Free parking in suburban areas and smaller towns. City centre disc parking system.

tolls.notes: M9 motorway (Waterford-Dublin) has toll at Waterford bypass. €1.90 per trip. Occasional Dublin M50 tolls if travelling to capital.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Free Travel Pass covers public transport but not car ownership costs. No specific car insurance or tax discounts for seniors.

transportation.walkability: Waterford city centre is compact and walkable, especially the Viking Triangle area. Tramore and Dunmore East have walkable village centres. Car essential for connecting towns and rural areas.

cycling.infrastructure: Waterford Greenway (46km) is one of Ireland's best cycling routes, connecting Waterford to Dungarvan. City cycling infrastructure improving but limited. No bike share scheme yet.
cycling.notes: Greenway is a major amenity for recreation. Flat terrain along river and coast makes cycling practical.

sources.title: Free Travel Pass - Dept. of Social Protection

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Hyundai Kona Electric ~€38K, Volkswagen ID.3 ~€35K, Tesla Model 3 ~€43K. SEAI grants available.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: SEAI grant up to €5,000 for new EV purchase. VRT (Vehicle Registration Tax) relief up to €5,000. Home charger grant €300 from SEAI. Toll reductions on M50.

chargingHome.notes: Electricity ~€0.25-0.35/kWh (among highest in EU). Night rate tariffs help. SEAI €300 home charger grant.

chargingPublic.notes: ESB eCars network. Ionity on motorways. Some free chargers at retail locations.

insurance.notes: Irish motor insurance among the most expensive in Europe. Shop around: AXA, Aviva, Liberty. EV premiums declining.

maintenance.notes: NCT (inspection) every 2 yrs after 4 yrs. Higher labor rates than continental Europe.

registration.notes: Motor tax for EVs: €120/year (lowest band). VRT relief up to €5,000.

electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: Strong EV demand in Ireland due to high fuel costs and government incentives. Good resale values.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging (€50), insurance (€80), maintenance (€42/mo avg), motor tax (€10/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.details: All public transport nationwide (Bus Éireann, Irish Rail, Dublin Bus, Luas, DART) free for 66+. Companion pass available for those who cannot travel alone.
Automatic entitlement to GP visit card for everyone aged 70+, regardless of income. Covers GP consultations free of charge.
For those with sufficient Irish PRSI contributions. Not applicable to US citizens unless they worked in Ireland. Non-contributory pension (means-tested) €266/week for residents.
For those aged 70+ (or 66+ if living alone). Free TV licence included. Must be resident in Ireland.
Additional tax credit for persons aged 65+. Married couple gets €490 combined.
DPS caps drug costs at €80/month for all. Over-70s with income under €1,050/week (single) qualify for full medical card — free prescriptions, hospital care, dental, optical.

plans.name: Vodafone Ireland (Local)
plans.notes: Vodafone Ireland SIM-only €10/mo per line (VAT included). Unlimited data, calls, texts within Ireland and EU roaming included. Three Ireland also competitive at €10-15/mo.

plans.name: T-Mobile US 55+ Essentials (US Number)
plans.notes: Keep T-Mobile US for banking/2FA/US calls. $55/mo for 2 lines (~€50). International roaming available but not designed for permanent use abroad — primarily for trips home.

housing.taxNotes: No stamp duty on residential leases. Tenancy registration with RTB (Residential Tenancies Board) mandatory — landlord responsibility. Rent Pressure Zones (RPZ) cap annual rent increases at 2% in designated areas. Waterford city is an RPZ. No VAT on residential rent.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month fixed term standard, then Part 4 tenancy rights (up to 6 years security of tenure). Security deposit typically 1 month rent. Pet deposits not standard but landlord may request. 28-day notice to terminate during first 6 months.
housing.marketNotes: Waterford rental market tighter than pre-2020 but much more available than Dublin. 2-bedroom houses available in city and surrounding towns. Older stone cottages and modern estates. Daft.ie and MyHome.ie are main listing sites.

stores.name: Aldi Ireland
stores.notes: German discounter, very popular in Ireland. Excellent value for groceries. Multiple locations in Waterford.

stores.name: Tesco Ireland
stores.notes: Largest supermarket chain in Ireland. Tesco Extra in Waterford Retail Park. Clubcard loyalty program with significant savings.

stores.name: Dunmore East Farmers Market
stores.notes: Seasonal coastal farmers market. Fresh fish, local produce, artisan foods. Summer weekends.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: HSE provides public healthcare. Private insurance (VHI/Laya) for faster access. Medical Card possible based on income. DPS caps drug costs at €80/month.

costs.notes: ~€230-270/mo. Community rating (no age loading in Ireland). Higher plan for ophthalmology.
Covered through public ophthalmology department. Long waiting list — private may be faster.
If public wait too long: €500-800/injection privately. Insurance may cover partially.
€50-65/visit, ~4/year. GP Visit Card may cover if income qualifies.
Private insurance covers consultant visits with excess/copay.
~€200-240/mo. Standard plan sufficient for diabetes management.
€50-65/visit for diabetes monitoring. Apply for GP Visit Card.
Long-Term Illness scheme covers diabetes supplies free.

shared.notes: PRSI dental benefits limited. Private dental ~€50-80/visit.
Free supplies for diabetes, thyroid conditions. Apply after residency.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Waterford has a growing African community (especially Nigerian and South African). Limited but quality options. Cork (1.5 hrs) and Dublin have more.

providers.name: Afroglam Hair Studio
providers.notes: Best local option for Afro hair. Braids, locs, weaves, color. Black-owned.

providers.name: Glam African Hair (Cork)
providers.notes: Larger salon with more specialists. Worth the trip for complex styles or color.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Waterford has several good salons on the Quay and in the city centre.

providers.name: Hairworks
providers.notes: Well-established Waterford salon. Color specialists. Highlights, grey blending.

providers.name: Peter Mark
providers.notes: Ireland's largest salon chain. Consistent quality. Good value for color services.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: Limited big & tall in Waterford. Penney's/Primark for budget. Dunnes Stores carries some extended sizes. Online from ASOS or Jacamo.

providers.name: Dunnes Stores
providers.notes: Irish retailer with extended sizes to 3XL. Good value menswear. Quality basics.

providers.name: Penney's (Primark)
providers.notes: Very affordable. Sizes to 3XL. Great for basics and casual wear.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Waterford city centre and City Square shopping centre. Good variety for a smaller city.

providers.name: Dunnes Stores
providers.notes: Strong women's section. Savida and Gallery lines. Irish sizes 8-22.

providers.name: Penney's (Primark)
providers.notes: Ultra-affordable fashion. Sizes 6-24. Good basics and trendy pieces.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Irish shoe sizing differs from US (use UK sizing + 1 for US). Wide widths available at Ecco and specialist stores.

providers.name: Ecco (Waterford)
providers.notes: Danish comfort shoes. Wide widths available. Good for walking. Men's and women's.

providers.name: Shoe Zone / Schuh
providers.notes: Budget to mid-range shoes. Casual, dress, athletic. Some extended sizes.
